rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ADA C. UNRUH FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Rulings: Case: Ada C. Unruh Family Trust ¿ 294184 Demurrer grounds & tentative rulings: 1. Failure to state a cause of action because the document which the Petition seeks to have determined to be a valid trust modification is unsigned, is not an ¿instrument¿ and cannot be a modification of a revocable living trust. Probate Code sections 45, 15401(a)(1), 15401(a)(2) and 15402. Tentative ruling ¿ sustain with 10 days leave to amend to clarify inconsistency between paragraphs 2 and 6 of the Petition. 2. The Petition fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action because it appears on its face that the action is barred by the Code of Civil Procedure sections 338(d) and 366.2. Tentative ruling ¿ sustain with 10 days leave to amend as to 338(d); overrule as to 366.2. 3. The Petition fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action because the document which the Petition seeks to have determined to be a valid trust modification does not comply with the Statute of Frauds, Probate Code Section 15206. Tentative ruling ¿ overrule. 4. The San Francisco Superior Court has already adjudicated that Marjory Halligan has no right of possession of the decedent¿s residence. Unruh v Halligan, San Francisco Superior Court Case No. CUD-08-624733, judgment entered February 25, 2008. The petition is barred by res judicata. Tentative ruling - overrule. 5. The San Francisco Superior Court has determined that Marjory Halligan was a care custodian of Ada C. Unruh. Unruh v Halligan, San Francisco Superior Court Case No. CUD-08-624733, transcript of the trial on February 25, 2008, which respondent will file with the Court and serve before the hearing of this demurrer. Tentative ruling ¿ overrule. If any party wishes to contest the tentative ruling, they must contact the probate courtroom clerk at (415) 551-3702 and opposing counsel no later than 4:00 p.m. on July 13, 2011.
